title: tectaria papyracea
intro: Tectaria papyracea, also known as the paper fern, is a species of plant in the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds resembling paper. Traditionally, Tectaria papyracea has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, Tectaria papyracea is often dried, crushed, and used in teas, infusions, or applied topically as a poultice to harness its medicinal properties.
